patch_rationale: Add canonical-cli-lint-precommit-installer.sh to substrate-registry on-demand allowlist (kind=scaffold). Script IS heavily wired (runtime via .flywheel/hooks/pre-commit-chain.sh + tested via tests/canonical-cli-lint-precommit.sh) but probe's runtime_source_corpus doesn't scan .flywheel/hooks/ or tests/ directories. Header marks 'flywheel-cli-surface: true' and 'Bead: flywheel-f0e77 (ldp0a follow-up)'. This is the 6th registry-allowlist bead this session.
design_notes: where field is STRING per flywheel-2xdi.60.1 Design Decision 3
kind=scaffold per 2xdi.72.1 precedent for installer-class scripts
lifecycle_state=active (installer is operator-on-demand AND wired in hooks chain)

consumers field lists BOTH runtime (.flywheel/hooks/pre-commit-chain.sh) AND test (tests/canonical-cli-lint-precommit.sh) — demonstrates the script is NOT orphan; probe's corpus 3 just doesn't see these paths

Sister harvest candidate (per faqj2 substrate-self-improving loop): extend runtime_source_corpus to scan .flywheel/hooks/*.sh + tests/*.sh. N=2 visible flagged scripts that are actually wired via tests/hooks (canonical-cli-lint-precommit-installer + fleet-coherence-classifiers); threshold N=4 not met yet

verification_post_import.gap_hunt_probe_post_apply: wired-but-cold flag cleared via on_demand_script_allowlist; underlying probe-corpus-too-narrow issue persists (4 other scripts still flagged similarly — faqj2 next-tick harvest candidate)
